overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a robust, highly targeted academic presence across Bluesky, LinkedIn, and Mastodon, with a collective following of 3,580. His content successfully targets the computational social science and disinformation research community, achieving a strong overall average engagement rate driven by major research milestones, hiring opportunities, and software releases.

contentThemes.description: Research updates and open-source tools (like CooRnet) designed to detect coordinated link sharing and platform manipulation.
Recruitment drives for postdoctoral researchers and PhD opportunities within computational social science.
Announcements of newly published academic papers, substantial research grants, and key conference presentations.

recommendations: Leverage LinkedIn's larger audience base by increasing post frequency to maximize overall visibility and reach.
Include direct hyperlinks to repositories and open-access papers within posts to turn high engagement rates into actionable traffic.
Utilize Bluesky's highly interactive academic network to foster discussion by posing open-ended questions alongside paper announcements.

platformInsights.bluesky: Serves as the most highly engaged channel relative to its size, yielding a 6.14% average engagement rate, proving particularly receptive to new research papers and doctoral opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: Reaches a smaller but highly specialized technical audience, generating a solid 4.55% average engagement rate for software updates like CooRnet.
platformInsights.linkedin: Functions as the main hub for professional announcements, delivering the highest absolute volume of engagement for career vacancies and institutional grant awards.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
